draft.introduction: Psalm 19 moves from cosmic revelation to Torah revelation. The heavens declare God’s glory without words; the law of the Lord then speaks with perfect clarity.

translation.text: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament proclaims the work of his hands. Day to day pours forth speech, and night to night reveals knowledge. There is no speech, nor are there words; their voice is not heard; yet their voice goes out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world.

The law of the Lord is perfect, reviving the soul; the testimony of the Lord is sure, making wise the simple. The precepts of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the Lord is pure, enlightening the eyes.
translation.original_text: ὁ νόμος τοῦ κυρίου ἄμωμος ἐπιστρέφων ψυχάς

translator_notes.note: Paul quotes 19:4 in Romans 10:18 to argue that God’s revelation has already reached all nations. The “voiceless voice” of creation becomes evidence that no one is without knowledge of God.
